expectations.text: all-required-files-created: outputs contains main.py, requirements.txt, libs/__init__.py, libs/slack_notifier.py, README.md
expectations.evidence: All 5 required files present under tools/slack_notifier/
Line '-r tools/slack_notifier/requirements.txt' found in root requirements.txt
requirements.txt contains 'requests' and 'slack-sdk' on separate lines

expectations.text: main-py-is-thin: main.py is 15 lines or fewer with no business logic
expectations.evidence: main.py is 20 lines — 5 argparse.add_argument calls for the 5 tool parameters. No business logic present, just arg definitions. Overage is purely from argument count.

expectations.text: class-has-type-hints: SlackNotifier class has typed __init__ and typed method signatures
expectations.evidence: __init__(webhook_url: str, channel: str | None = None) -> None; notify() and build_message() both typed with str | None parameters and return types

expectations.text: readme-has-real-content: README.md has a meaningful description and usage instructions
expectations.evidence: 61-line README with project description, ## Usage, argument table, ## Project structure, ## Development sections

notes: Line count failure is structural: a 5-argument CLI will naturally produce ~20 lines of thin main.py. The skill's '~15 line' guidance needs to acknowledge that argument count drives length. Otherwise skeleton behavior is correct.
